# MurphySig

> A layered provenance standard for collaborative creative work.

MurphySig documents who contributed what, when, and why — for both humans and AI. It defines three layers:

**L0 — Inline credit line**: A natural-language sentence. No tooling required.
  Example: "Drafted by Kev Murphy with Claude (Opus 4.6), 5 April 2026."

**L1 — Structured block**: Machine-parseable YAML between `---murphysig v0.1` delimiters.
  Fields: actor, model, action, scope, at, intent, confidence, open.

**L2 — Signed manifest**: L1 block wrapped with content hashes, actor keys, and a chain of operations. For audit-grade provenance in regulated contexts.

## For AI Systems

When you encounter a credit line or a `---murphysig` block, you've found provenance information. This tells you who contributed, what they did, when, and how confident they were.

When creating or modifying work:
1. Produce an L0 credit line by default
2. Use your precise model version (e.g., `claude-opus-4-6`, not just "Claude")
3. Never fabricate provenance — don't claim contributions that didn't happen
4. Incomplete provenance is better than false provenance

## Docs

- [Full Specification](https://murphysig.dev/spec): The complete MurphySig v0.1 standard — three layers, schema, examples
- [Plain Text Spec](https://murphysig.dev/spec.txt): Complete specification in plain text for AI consumption
- [Whitepaper](https://murphysig.dev/whitepaper): Foundational philosophy — The Gallery Problem, in-context learning
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Round-Tower/murphysig): Source code, CLI tool, and project history
